logistics.gettingThere: Train Oslo S → Dombås on the Dovrebanen (SJ Norge), Friday 10 July. Morning departures roughly 08:00 (arr ~12:09) and 10:00; journey ~4 h. Reserve bike space when you book at entur.no — slots are limited (~5 per train) and sell out in July. You ride from Dombås the same afternoon. (A Røros start adds ~4 wilderness days and does not fit a Sunday-19 fly-out; it would need ~12 field days.)
logistics.gettingBack: Two scenic legs, both need a bike reservation. (1) Flåm → Bergen by Norled express boat through the Sognefjord: departs Flåm 15:30, arrives Bergen ~20:45 (≈5.5 h via Aurland, Balestrand, Vik), adult ~NOK 1,295. Bikes travel on the open aft deck for a small fee but space is limited — confirm with Norled at booking. Your Flåm morning is free. Overnight Bergen. (2) Bergen → Oslo on the Bergensbanen next day, ~7 h, several daytime departures (first ~06:21, then ~07:58 / 10:58 / 15:35); flat NOK 275 summer bike fee, 15 reservable spaces. It climbs back over Hardangervidda past Finse, so you see the Rallarvegen from the train. Overnight Oslo, fly Sunday 19.
extraNotes: Book early (you are inside 4 weeks): the Gjende boat to Memurubu (gjende.no, mandatory, sells out in July), Gjendesheim beds, the Flåm→Bergen express boat with bike space (norled.no), the Bergensbanen bike reservation (vy.no), and Bergen + Oslo hotels.

Bergen overnight: stay near Bryggen / the station so you can roll to the train; bike-friendly options include Marken Gjestehus (hostel) and the Citybox hotels. Oslo overnight before the flight: Citybox or Comfort near Oslo S keeps you one train stop from the airport express.

Optional Flåm-morning side quests before the 15:30 boat (flagged on Day 8): ride or shuttle up to the Stegastein viewpoint (Bjørgavegen, ~12 km and 600 m up from Aurland — or take the FjordSafari/bus), or a short Nærøyfjord RIB/kayak. Keep an eye on the clock; the boat does not wait.
Jotunheimvegen / Slådalsvegen tolls: free for cyclists (number-plate cameras bill cars only).

Water + food on each plateau crossing (Slådalsvegen, Sjodalen, Valdresflye, Slettefjell, Stølsvidda, Rallarvegen): carry 1.5 L+ and half a day of food; streams are plentiful but treat or avoid those near grazing herds.

DNT membership: worth it if you take hut beds or hut dinners (Gjendesheim is a DNT lodge). Lock strategy: one mid-weight lock shared between two bikes; Flåm is the one real theft node, use the Visitor Centre lockers for bags.

Vinjerock festival runs 16–19 July 2026 at Eidsbugarden near Bygdin (sold out) — expect a livelier Jotunheimen late in the week. Rallarvegen status: check Rallarvegrapporten (rallarvegen.com) the week before; 2026 carries more snow than normal at Finse height.
Sunday 12 July is the Besseggen day for the recommended plan, so groceries are shut anyway — you run on Vågå supplies plus the Gjendesheim lodge and Memurubu café.

besseggen.label: Dombås + Besseggen
besseggen.sub: ≈417 km · 6 ride days + the hike · 9 → 19 July

stages.summary: Fly into Oslo, overnight in town. Build the bikes, buy fuel canisters (not allowed on the plane), and pre-book tomorrow's Dombås train bike spaces if you haven't.
stages.notes: Last easy chance for stove gas, spares and a big dinner.

stages.surface: gravel: Slådalsvegen toll road, paved descent to Vågåmo
stages.summary: Morning train Oslo → Dombås (~4 h), shop, then straight onto Slådalsvegen: 32 km of premier gravel over the wild-reindeer plateau at ~1,130 m, finishing with a 750 m drop into Vågåmo.
stages.notes: Afternoon-start transit day, so it's short. Buy lunch + dinner in Dombås (after 11:00 even on a Sunday).

stages.summary: Climb Fv51 up Sjodalen past Lemonsjøen and Maurvangen to Gjendesheim (990 m) at the foot of Besseggen. A short, scenic positioning day — arrive with the afternoon free.
stages.notes: Book / confirm tomorrow's ~08:45 Gjende boat to Memurubu tonight. Bikes: only an unlocked ski room at Gjendesheim — lock them and strip valuables.

stages.summary: Boat to Memurubu, then hike the Besseggen ridge back over Veslfjellet (1,743 m) — green Lake Gjende 400 m directly below blue Bessvatnet. Norway's most famous day hike. No riding.
stages.notes: Easy scrambling, fine in trail shoes in good weather — skip and ride on if it's storming. Memurubu has a café; Gjendesheim does dinner + packed lunch.

stages.surface: Fv51 over Valdresflye (1,389 m), then Mjølkevegen gravel over Slettefjell
stages.summary: The big one. Over the Valdresflye pass (1,389 m, the trip's high point) to Beitostølen, then join Mjølkevegen and climb Slettefjell (~1,240 m) before the run to Vaset's summer-farm country.
stages.notes: Longest riding day, the day after the hike — pace it. Bail option: overnight Beitostølen (km 38) if the legs are cooked, then a longer Day 5.

stages.surface: gravel across Stølsvidda, tarmac descent into Gol
stages.summary: Gentle pastoral riding across Stølsvidda — Norway's densest summer-farm landscape — past Tisleifjorden, then a long descent off the plateau to Gol, the Mjølkevegen terminus.
stages.notes: Recovery day. Gol is full-service: supermarkets, Intersport (gas + dehydrated meals), cheap hotels.

stages.surface: cycle path + riverside gravel (Route 4) to Geilo; Rv7 to Haugastøl
stages.summary: Up Hallingdal on National Cycle Route 4 through Torpo and Ål to Geilo (794 m), then the Rv7 to Haugastøl at 988 m — km 0 of the Rallarvegen.
stages.notes: Rv7 Geilo→Haugastøl is fast traffic with no cycle path — hop the train/bus for that 22 km if you'd rather. Geilo is the last big resupply + bike shop before Flåm.

stages.summary: The finale, and it's all on the car-free Rallarvegen: up to Finse (1,222 m, highest station in Norway) under the Hardangerjøkulen glacier, over the 1,322 m crest at Fagernut (waffles), then the 21 hairpins of Myrdalsberget and the green Flåm valley to the fjord.
stages.notes: Long but net-downhill after Finse. Optional 30-min stops: Rallarmuseet at Finse, the glacier viewpoint. Reach Flåm tonight; Ægir BrewPub for the finish beer.

stages.summary: Free morning in Flåm, then the 15:30 Norled express boat down the Sognefjord to Bergen (arr ~20:45). One of the world's great fjord rides. Overnight Bergen.
highlights: Optional AM side quest: ride/shuttle to Stegastein viewpoint
Optional AM side quest: short Nærøyfjord RIB or kayak
Sognefjord by boat: Aurland, Balestrand, Vik
stages.notes: Optional adventure window in the morning (Stegastein or Nærøyfjord). Be at the Flåm quay by 15:00 with a bike reservation — the boat does not wait.

stages.summary: Bergen → Oslo on the Bergensbanen, ~7 h and arguably the most beautiful train ride in Europe: it climbs back over Hardangervidda and passes Finse, so you watch the Rallarvegen roll by from a warm seat. Overnight Oslo.
highlights: Finse + the line you just rode
Reserve a left-side window seat (Bergen→Oslo) for the fjord side early on
stages.notes: Book the bike space (flat NOK 275 summer). Take a morning departure (~07:58 or 10:58) to reach Oslo with the evening free.

stages.summary: Fly out of Oslo. Flytoget airport express from Oslo S is ~20 min; box or bag the bikes the night before.

gravel.label: Dombås, all-gravel (no hike)
gravel.sub: ≈443 km · 6 ride days + Flåm day · 9 → 19 July

stages.summary: Fly into Oslo, overnight in town. Build the bikes, buy fuel canisters, pre-book the Dombås train bike spaces.
stages.notes: Last easy chance for stove gas, spares and a big dinner.

stages.summary: Morning train Oslo → Dombås, then Slådalsvegen: 32 km of premier gravel over the wild-reindeer plateau (~1,130 m) and a 750 m drop to Vågåmo.
stages.notes: Afternoon-start transit day. Buy lunch + dinner in Dombås.

stages.surface: paved Fv51 to Randsverk, then gravel: Murudalen + Jotunheimvegen along the Vinstre lakes
stages.summary: The hardest day, and the most Mjølkevegen of them all: 600 m climb out of Vågåmo, regroup at Randsverk, then gravel through Murudalen onto the Vinstre lakes with the whole Jotunheimen skyline ahead.
stages.notes: Hard day (90 km / 1,750 m) — start early. Coop Marked Randsverk is the only shop; buy tomorrow's breakfast. Wild camping by Vinstre is the no-booking fallback.

stages.surface: gravel: Jotunheimvegen, Bygdin, Fv51 to Beitostølen, Mjølkevegen over Slettefjell
stages.summary: Finish the Vinstre lakes to Bygdin (M/B Bitihorn, café), drop to Beitostølen, then Mjølkevegen over Slettefjell (~1,240 m) to Vaset's summer-farm country.
stages.notes: Sunday: groceries shut except where noted — lean on Beitostølen's late-opening shops and lodge food.

stages.surface: gravel across Stølsvidda, descent to Gol
stages.summary: Pastoral riding across Stølsvidda past Tisleifjorden, then the long descent to Gol, the Mjølkevegen terminus.
stages.notes: Recovery day. Gol is full-service.

stages.surface: Route 4 to Geilo, Rv7 to Haugastøl
stages.summary: Up Hallingdal through Torpo and Ål to Geilo, then Rv7 to Haugastøl — km 0 of the Rallarvegen.
stages.notes: Geilo is the last big resupply + bike shop before Flåm. Train/bus option for the Rv7 stretch.

stages.summary: The car-free Rallarvegen all the way: up to Finse (1,222 m) under the Hardangerjøkulen glacier, over the 1,322 m crest at Fagernut, then the 21 hairpins of Myrdalsberget down to the fjord at Flåm.
stages.notes: Net-downhill after Finse. Reach Flåm tonight — you've a spare day tomorrow.

stages.summary: Earned rest day in Flåm. This is where the all-gravel plan spends the day Besseggen would have taken.
stages.notes: SIDE QUEST day: pick a fjord activity (book the Nærøyfjord cruise ahead). Or just rest the legs before the journey home.

stages.summary: The 15:30 Norled express boat down the Sognefjord to Bergen (arr ~20:45). Free morning in Flåm. Overnight Bergen.
highlights: Sognefjord by boat: Aurland, Balestrand, Vik
stages.notes: Be at the quay by 15:00 with a bike reservation.

stages.summary: Bergen → Oslo on the Bergensbanen, ~7 h over Hardangervidda past Finse — you watch the Rallarvegen from the train. Overnight Oslo.
stages.notes: Book the bike space; morning departure to free up the Oslo evening.

stages.summary: Fly out of Oslo. Flytoget airport express from Oslo S is ~20 min; box the bikes the night before.
